V-M-075.scope: Phase-57/Phase-59 premium user cabinet evidence remains valid. Phase-62 verification now defines the compactness and deletion-audit contract for user cabinet, public/auth adjacency, compact UI invariants, responsive regressions, preserved primary workflows, redaction, and protected backend/deploy/runtime isolation.

module-checks.check-11: Static route proof emitted [PremiumUserCabinet][phase57][SCREENSHOT_MATRIX_REVIEWED] static-route-proof; authenticated Chrome screenshots remain clean-deploy follow-up because local backend session fixture is not materialized.
module-checks.check-12: python3 XML parse for docs/modules/M-075.xml docs/plans/Phase-57.xml docs/verification/V-M-075.xml docs/verification-index.xml
module-checks.check-15: git diff --name-only HEAD -- install.sh docker-compose.yml nginx deploy deploy/mtproto-de-compose.yml .env.example backend/app backend/tests mtproto-runtime official-mtproxy was empty
module-checks.check-23: python3 -m xml.etree.ElementTree docs/plans/Phase-62.xml docs/modules/M-075.xml docs/verification/V-M-075.xml docs/verification-index.xml
module-checks.check-24: mygrace lint; git diff --check; protected diff guard for backend/deploy/runtime/install surfaces
module-checks.check-27: node scripts/phase71-device-config-master-detail-smoke.mjs asserts /dashboard/config frameless export actions and mobile copy-action nowrap markers.

V-M-075.evidence: Phase-57 implementation added premium protected user command center, /dashboard/* primary actions, compact config/subscription/referrals/settings surfaces, M-075 CSS primitives, and scripts/phase57-premium-user-cabinet-smoke.mjs. Frontend build and Phase-45/46/48/50/52/53/55/56 regression smokes passed. Phase-59 then added bounded copy/download/proxy feedback and status transitions; frontend build, Phase-59 smoke, Phase-57 regression smoke, and post-commit Phase-58 regression smoke passed. No backend API, billing catalog, VPN/MTProto runtime, deploy/install, nginx, docker-compose, or secret-handling surfaces were changed.

Phase-62 compactness/deletion audit folded dashboard traffic/server/device diagnostics, config import guidance/config diagnostics, subscription included/tariff details, and public feature/tariff detail copy into compact secondary panels while preserving VPN, subscription, MTProto, auth, password recovery, tariff, redaction, and responsive workflows. Frontend build, Phase-62 smoke, Phase-57/56/61/60 regressions, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff --check, and protected backend/deploy/runtime guard passed.

Phase-68 removed the dashboard command center and secondary fold, moved subscription/tariffs/calendar into a shared SubscriptionPanel below the MTProto card, kept /dashboard/subscription compatible, removed visible config raw/diagnostics, updated smokes, and passed user/admin builds plus Phase-45/46/50/53/57/58/60/61/62/63/67 regression gates.
Phase-69 updated /dashboard/referrals so recent invite rows render masked referred identity instead of generic "Реферал #N"; frontend build, Phase-69 smoke, and Phase-57 regression smoke passed.

Phase-70 retuned /dashboard/config QR rendering to level M with margin, kept QR/download/copy/device actions, kept config-diagnostics/raw-config absent, and passed frontend build plus Phase-70/57/62 smokes.

classification.remove: Duplicated marketing copy inside authenticated surfaces, redundant explanatory blocks, decorative-only panels, stale helper text, and repeated status cards that do not expose a unique action or state.
classification.collapse: Secondary instructions, raw config help, long referral/history explanations, and low-frequency account details that can live behind details/tabs without hiding primary workflows.
classification.move-secondary: Verbose public/auth explanations, referral history, long setup notes, and non-critical troubleshooting hints when they compete with first-screen VPN, subscription, or MTProto actions.
classification.compact: Dashboard, config, subscription, referrals, settings, auth verification, recovery, and tariff surfaces using denser rows, bounded blocks, shorter labels, and mobile-first action grouping.
classification.keep: VPN QR/download/copy/raw config access, device create/rotate/revoke, MTProto tg:// and https://t.me links, subscription countdown/calendar/tariffs, referrals copy flow, password/security settings, email-proof/recovery clarity, logout, and redaction.

preservation-invariants.invariant-1: VPN, subscription, MTProto, devices, referrals, and settings remain reachable within one or two taps on phone layouts.
preservation-invariants.invariant-2: Public/auth compaction must not imply access before verified email, hide password policy examples, hide duplicate-email recovery CTA, or restore stale three-day trial wording.
preservation-invariants.invariant-3: Matrix/cyberpunk visuals remain background/system styling and must not intercept pointer, scroll, form, calendar, copy, download, or Telegram-link interactions.
preservation-invariants.invariant-4: Phase-62 must not change backend APIs, billing catalog ownership, VPN/MTProto runtime, deploy/install scripts, nginx, docker-compose, secrets, or production topology.

verification-matrix.target-1: Protected /dashboard route tree remains under Phase-56 route split and all user cabinet routes keep phone-safe navigation, visible current account state, logout, and primary actions.
verification-matrix.target-2: Dashboard first viewport surfaces VPN state/config entry, subscription countdown or pending-first-VPN state, MTProto proxy status/actions, and device/account context without bulky stacked panels or horizontal scroll.
verification-matrix.target-3: Config page preserves QR, .conf download, copy, raw config, device create/rotate/revoke, and Phase-48 application/octet-stream .conf download guarantees.
verification-matrix.target-4: Subscription page preserves server-derived days/hours/minutes, active date range/calendar, pending first-VPN wording, canonical M-068 tariffs, and plan_id-only checkout shape.
verification-matrix.target-5: MTProto owner-only tg:// app link, https://t.me browser link, Russian labels, bounded 30s refresh, pending/degraded states, and no-secret console/log guards remain intact.
verification-matrix.target-6: Referrals and settings are compact secondary surfaces that preserve referral copy action, password strength validation, profile/password controls, Russian-only user-cabinet policy, and no localStorage/token leaks.
verification-matrix.target-7: Phone/tablet/desktop/reduced-motion screenshots show no text/icon/button/status overlap, no hidden primary CTA, and no Matrix layer click/scroll interception.

success-scenarios.scenario-1: VPN config, QR/download/copy, subscription renewal, MTProto copy/open, and device controls are reachable within one or two taps on phone.
success-scenarios.scenario-2: MTProto owner-only actions remain visible, compact, localized, dynamically refreshed, and redacted from smoke output and console traces.
success-scenarios.scenario-3: Trial/countdown/calendar/tariff UI stays server-derived and never reintroduces stale 3-day copy or frontend-owned payment amounts.
success-scenarios.scenario-4: Referrals and settings retain their workflows while adopting compact premium Matrix surfaces.
success-scenarios.scenario-5: Phase-57 does not touch backend API shape, billing catalog, VPN/MTProto runtime, deploy/install scripts, nginx, docker-compose, or secret handling.

failure-scenarios.failure-1: Premium layout hides QR/download/copy/raw config, trial countdown, tariff state, MTProto link, referral copy, settings forms, or device controls.
failure-scenarios.failure-2: Critical values become clipped, unreadable, or hidden behind Matrix canvas/scanline surfaces on phone/tablet/desktop screenshots.
failure-scenarios.failure-3: Smoke, console markers, screenshots, or docs print full VPN configs, QR data, proxy secrets, tg links, bearer/refresh tokens, raw emails, payment IDs, or API keys.
failure-scenarios.failure-4: Phase-57 changes backend API shape, checkout payload ownership, billing catalog values, MTProto/VPN runtime behavior, deploy/install scripts, nginx, docker-compose, or production topology.
failure-scenarios.failure-5: User cabinet becomes a decorative landing/dashboard surface and requires excessive scrolling for the primary VPN, subscription, MTProto, or device workflows.

telemetry-requirements.requirement-1: Allowed smoke output: route names, viewport names, selector markers, redacted state names, screenshot paths, and pass/fail markers.
telemetry-requirements.requirement-2: Forbidden smoke output: full VPN configs, QR payloads, WireGuard private keys, MTProto ee secrets, tg:// or https://t.me proxy links with secrets, bearer/refresh tokens, reset/verify tokens, real user emails, raw IP history, payment IDs, YooKassa/Resend keys, or API keys.
telemetry-requirements.requirement-3: If screenshots require authenticated fixtures, fixtures must use synthetic emails and synthetic data only; live clean-deploy screenshots must be operator-approved and redacted.

phase-gates.gate-1: M-075 smoke proves dashboard/config/subscription/referrals/settings route markers, primary actions, compact layout primitives, and redaction guards.
phase-gates.gate-2: M-036, M-045, M-063, M-064, M-068, M-070, M-071, M-072 regression smokes remain satisfied after user cabinet edits.
phase-gates.gate-3: Frontend build, Phase-57 smoke, regression smokes, screenshot matrix, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff check, and protected deploy/backend/runtime guard pass before Phase-57 can be marked done-local.
phase-gates.gate-4: Phase-62 passed locally on 2026-06-04: inventory, classification, prune/collapse decisions, preserved workflow proof, static-route proof, frontend build, Phase-62 smoke, Phase-57/56/61/60 regressions, XML parse, MyGRACE lint, git diff check, and protected backend/deploy/runtime guard passed.
phase-gates.gate-5: Authenticated screenshots may use static-route proof locally; live clean-deploy authenticated screenshot review remains required before production-signoff claims.

V-M-075.autonomous-execution-safety: Safe for local autonomous execution if changes stay inside frontend user/public/auth routes, shared frontend CSS, Phase-62 smoke verification, and MyGRACE docs. Stop execution if a backend API change, billing catalog mutation, VPN/MTProto runtime change, deploy/install/nginx/docker-compose change, or secret-handling change appears necessary.

phase-68-verification-design.scope: User dashboard compaction, MTProto card copy, subscription merge into /dashboard, tariff/calendar presentation, user-shell logo treatment, and compact animated cabinet controls.
phase-68-verification-design.gate-1: /dashboard must not render phase57-command-center or phase62-secondary-fold.
phase-68-verification-design.gate-2: MTProto card must show the approved Russian title, remove the old helper copy, preserve tg:// and https://t.me links, and keep owner-only secret redaction.
phase-68-verification-design.gate-3: Subscription content must appear below MTProto on /dashboard while /dashboard/subscription remains route-compatible and non-broken.
phase-68-verification-design.gate-4: Tariff cards must use KrotPN Self, KrotPN Family, and KrotPN Team names with approved short descriptions and no "Детали тарифа" block.
phase-68-verification-design.gate-5: Subscription calendar must be compact, labelled "Календарь подписки", and make cross-month active ranges understandable without visual bulk.
phase-68-verification-design.commands: cd frontend && npm run build; node scripts/phase57-premium-user-cabinet-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase62-compactness-deletion-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase68-dashboard-subscription-smoke.mjs
phase-68-verification-design.redaction: Smoke output must not print full MTProto secrets, full VPN configs, bearer tokens, or real user emails.

phase-69-verification-design.scope: Referral history identity rendering inside the premium user cabinet.
phase-69-verification-design.gate-1: Recent invite rows must render "Реферал masked-email" and not "Реферал #N".
phase-69-verification-design.gate-2: Full referred email, payment IDs, backend user IDs, tokens, and hidden bonus internals must not be rendered in user-visible rows or smoke output.
phase-69-verification-design.commands: cd frontend && npm run build; node scripts/phase69-referral-bonus-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase57-premium-user-cabinet-smoke.mjs

phase-70-verification-design.scope: Config page compaction and QR display parity from the user cabinet surface.
phase-70-verification-design.gate-1: /dashboard/config must not render config-diagnostics or data-phase57-raw-config blocks.
phase-70-verification-design.gate-2: QR modal must remain reachable, readable, and copy/download actions must continue to work after compacting the page.
phase-70-verification-design.gate-3: Visual QR simplification is allowed only after payload parity with backend and CLI is proven by M-003/M-022 gates.
phase-70-verification-design.commands: cd frontend && npm run build; node scripts/phase70-config-qr-parity-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase62-compactness-deletion-smoke.mjs

phase-71-verification-design.scope: User cabinet config master-detail and requested polish verification.
phase-71-verification-design.gate-1: /dashboard/config must show compact device slots, selected-device detail, QR/.conf/Copy primary actions, and no raw config/diagnostic panel return.
phase-71-verification-design.gate-2: Rotate and delete must be secondary confirmation-safe actions, not adjacent to the primary export controls.
phase-71-verification-design.gate-3: QR modal must use an icon-only X close button and must not show the removed AmneziaVPN scan instruction.
phase-71-verification-design.gate-4: Desktop Выйти ordering, Device-bound label replacement, subscription helper-copy removal, and Settings English i18n cleanup must pass static smoke.
phase-71-verification-design.commands: cd frontend && npm run build; node scripts/phase71-device-config-master-detail-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase57-premium-user-cabinet-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase62-compactness-deletion-smoke.mjs

V-M-075.phase-71-evidence: Frontend build and Phase-71 smoke passed for config master-detail, secondary destructive actions, QR close icon, device label replacement, backend-time copy removal, settings i18n, and Phase-62 collapse marker preservation.

phase-72-verification-design.scope: Premium user cabinet verification for logout placement, mobile navigation, Settings language deletion, and compact calendar.
phase-72-verification-design.gate-1: Desktop cabinet navigation must show Settings followed by Logout and must keep Logout visible without moving it into low-priority footer copy.
phase-72-verification-design.gate-2: Mobile cabinet navigation must keep core routes reachable in one tap and must not cover primary actions.
phase-72-verification-design.gate-3: Settings must no longer expose language selection controls, language labels, or untranslated English/Russian mode toggles.
phase-72-verification-design.gate-4: Calendar compaction must preserve subscription date meaning and not make the dashboard feel bulky.
phase-72-verification-design.commands: cd frontend && npm run build; node scripts/phase57-premium-user-cabinet-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase72-user-auth-navigation-smoke.mjs

V-M-075.phase-72-evidence: Frontend build and Phase-72/57/61/68/71 smokes passed for desktop logout under Settings, mobile dock reachability, Russian-only Settings removal, and compact subscription calendar density.

phase-73-verification-design.scope: Premium user config page verification for KPN copy, truthful Amnezia guidance, selected-device exports, and tariff-aware limit copy.
phase-73-verification-design.gate-1: /dashboard/config must use "KPN Конфигурация", replace "Новый конфиг" with "Новый конфиг для вашего устройства", and must not show "QR, .conf и копирование теперь привязаны к выбранному устройству."
phase-73-verification-design.gate-2: AmneziaVPN QR must not be presented as a working scan path; AmneziaVPN users must be guided to download and import the selected-device .conf file.
phase-73-verification-design.gate-3: AmneziaWG QR, .conf download, Copy, selected-device switching, create-disabled-at-limit, and secondary rotate/delete actions remain compact and reachable.
phase-73-verification-design.gate-4: Device-limit exhausted copy must include the current tariff display name and must not claim a wrong plan or expose backend-only billing internals.
phase-73-verification-design.gate-5: Protected backend, billing, VPN runtime, MTProto runtime, deploy/install, nginx, docker-compose, create-client.sh, and admin source surfaces remain untouched unless explicitly approved by a later phase.
phase-73-verification-design.commands: cd frontend && npm run build; node scripts/phase73-config-amnezia-qr-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase48-config-download-mime-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase57-premium-user-cabinet-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase70-config-qr-parity-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase71-device-config-master-detail-smoke.mjs; protected deploy/runtime/admin guard.

phase-73-evidence.summary: /dashboard/config uses KPN copy, no longer advertises unsupported AmneziaVPN QR, preserves selected-device AmneziaWG QR/.conf/Copy, guides AmneziaVPN users to .conf import, and shows display-only tariff-aware device-limit copy.

phase-76-verification-design.scope: Premium user cabinet regression guard for the consolidated frontend polish gate.
phase-76-verification-design.gate-1: Dashboard, Config, Subscription, Referrals, and Settings must remain compact, Russian-only, and action-reachable after Phase-72/73 changes.
phase-76-verification-design.gate-2: Config page must preserve selected-device QR/.conf/Copy, AmneziaVPN .conf guidance, KPN copy, and tariff-aware limit messaging.
phase-76-verification-design.gate-3: Phase-57, Phase-62, Phase-68, Phase-70, Phase-71, Phase-72, and Phase-73 smokes must remain green or record an explicit blocker.
phase-76-verification-design.commands: node scripts/phase57-premium-user-cabinet-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase62-compactness-deletion-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase68-dashboard-subscription-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase70-config-qr-parity-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase71-device-config-master-detail-smoke.mjs; Phase-72/73 smokes.

V-M-075.phase-76-evidence: Consolidated Phase-76 gate passed for premium user cabinet: Phase-57/62/68/70/71 regressions plus Phase-72/73 smokes preserved dashboard, config, subscription, referrals, Settings cleanup, selected-device exports, Amnezia guidance, and tariff-limit messaging.

phase-77-verification-design.scope: Premium user cabinet verification for subscription copy/link/calendar polish and fixed mobile navigation.
phase-77-verification-design.gate-1: Dashboard/subscription surfaces must show active-until copy and clickable config guidance without reintroducing bulky helper panels.
phase-77-verification-design.gate-2: Calendar role pulses must remain compact and not make the dashboard visually bulky.
phase-77-verification-design.gate-3: Mobile dock must remain visible while preserving one/two-tap access to config, subscription, referrals, and settings.

V-M-075.phase-77-evidence: Phase-77 preserved premium user cabinet workflows while adding active-until copy, config link, calendar role markers, and fixed mobile dock; Phase-57 and Phase-68 regressions passed.

mobile-scroll-dock-fix-verification.scope: Premium user cabinet regression for mobile scroll from the MTProto card and persistent bottom navigation.
mobile-scroll-dock-fix-verification.gate-1: MTProto secret presentation remains readable and copyable without trapping vertical page scroll on touch devices.
mobile-scroll-dock-fix-verification.gate-2: Mobile bottom navigation remains visible after route changes and keeps one-tap access to dashboard, config, subscription, referrals, and settings.
mobile-scroll-dock-fix-verification.commands: cd frontend && npm run build; node scripts/phase77-subscription-mobile-polish-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase57-premium-user-cabinet-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase68-dashboard-subscription-smoke.mjs.

config-action-polish-verification.scope: Premium user config export buttons: QR, .conf, and Copy.
config-action-polish-verification.gate-1: /dashboard/config export action group must not render the extra dark sticky-action frame around QR/.conf/Copy.
config-action-polish-verification.gate-2: Mobile "Копировать конфиг" action must remain on one line and must not split the Russian word at narrow widths.
config-action-polish-verification.gate-3: QR/.conf/Copy handlers, selected-device behavior, and AmneziaVPN .conf guidance remain unchanged.
config-action-polish-verification.commands: cd frontend && npm run build; node scripts/phase71-device-config-master-detail-smoke.mjs; node scripts/phase73-config-amnezia-qr-smoke.mjs; protected backend/deploy/runtime/admin guard.

V-M-075.config-action-polish-evidence: Config.tsx exposes [ConfigPage][fix][EXPORT_ACTIONS_FRAMELESS] and [MobileUserCabinet][fix][COPY_CONFIG_NO_WRAP]; Phase-71 smoke validates the markers and route-local CSS.
